chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

tests.test_name: Dual Nature of Radiation & Matter Test 01

questions.question_text: According to Einstein's photoelectric equation, the plot of the kinetic energy of the emitted photoelectrons from a metal versus the frequencey of the incident radiation gives a straight line, whose slope:
questions.question_html: According to Einstein's photoelectric equation, the plot of the kinetic energy of the emitted photoelectrons from a metal versus the frequencey of the incident radiation gives a straight line, whose slope:

options.text: depends both on the intensity of the radiation and the metal used
options.html: depends both on the intensity of the radiation and the metal used

options.text: is the same for all metals and independent of the intensity of the radiation
options.html: is the same for all metals and independent of the intensity of the radiation

options.text: depends on the nature of the metal used
options.html: depends on the nature of the metal used

options.text: depends on the intensity of the radiation
options.html: depends on the intensity of the radiation

questions.correct_option_text: is the same for all metals and independent of the intensity of the radiation
questions.explanation:
According to Einstein's photoelectric equation, {tex}h
u=h
u_0+ rac12mv_{max^2}{/tex} or {tex} rac12mv_{max^2}=h
u-h
u_0{/tex} ...(i) The equation of a straight line in intercept form is given by y = mx + c ...(ii) Comparing the equations (i) and (ii), we get m = h Therefore, slope of the plot is equal to Planck's constant. Hence, the plot is same for all metals and independent of the intensity of the radiation.

questions.question_text: In which case is electron emission from a metal not known?
questions.question_html: In which case is electron emission from a metal not known?

options.text: Illuminating a metal surface with suitable light
options.html: Illuminating a metal surface with suitable light

options.text: Applying a very strong magnetic field to a metal
options.html: Applying a very strong magnetic field to a metal

options.text: Applying a very strong electric field to a metal
options.html: Applying a very strong electric field to a metal

questions.correct_option_text: Applying a very strong magnetic field to a metal
questions.explanation: Photo emission, thermo emission and electric field emission are known. Emission of electrons due to magnetic field is not known.

options.text: emission of electrons by glass when illuminated by light of suitable frequency
options.html: emission of electrons by glass when illuminated by light of suitable frequency

options.text: emission of electrons by insulators when illuminated by light of suitable frequency
options.html: emission of electrons by insulators when illuminated by light of suitable frequency

options.text: emission of electrons by metals when illuminated by light of suitable frequency
options.html: emission of electrons by metals when illuminated by light of suitable frequency

options.text: emission of electrons by metals when heated
options.html: emission of electrons by metals when heated

questions.correct_option_text: emission of electrons by metals when illuminated by light of suitable frequency
questions.explanation: When the light strikes on a metal surface, electrons on the surface of the metal get energy from the light and get emitted from the surface. It is found that there will be no photoemission from a metal surface below a certain frequency of light. The phenomenon of emission of photoelectron from a metal surface by light of suitable frequency is called photoelectric effect.

options.text: with both visible and UV light depending on the metal
options.html: with both visible and UV light depending on the metal

questions.correct_option_text: with both visible and UV light depending on the metal
questions.explanation: Emission of photo-electron takes place when energy of incident photon is greater than that of work function of material. Work function is different for different material. Photoelectric effect takes place for both UV and visible light depending on work function.

options.text: light is converted into electrical energy
options.html: light is converted into electrical energy

options.text: electrical energy is converted into light energy
options.html: electrical energy is converted into light energy

options.text: electrical energy is converted magnetic field energy
options.html: electrical energy is converted magnetic field energy

options.text: electrical energy is converted into heat
options.html: electrical energy is converted into heat

questions.correct_option_text: light is converted into electrical energy
questions.explanation: In photoelectric effect, emission of photo-electron takes place so that photo-current flows in circuit.

questions.question_text: In the experimental set up for studying photoelectric effect, if keeping the frequency of the incident radiation and the accelerating potential fixed, the intensity of light is varied, then
questions.question_html: In the experimental set up for studying photoelectric effect, if keeping the frequency of the incident radiation and the accelerating potential fixed, the intensity of light is varied, then
questions.explanation: With increase in intensity, no. of photons incident increases. So more electrons emitted from metal and photo current increases with increase in intensity.

questions.question_text: Sodium and copper have work function 2.3 eV and 4.5 eV respectively. Then, the ratio of the wavelengths is nearest to :
questions.question_html: Sodium and copper have work function 2.3 eV and 4.5 eV respectively. Then, the ratio of the wavelengths is nearest to :
questions.explanation: Here, {tex}\omega_{Cu\;}=4.5eV{/tex} and {tex}\omega_{Na\;}=2.3eV{/tex} Let {tex}\lambda_{Cu}{/tex} and {tex}\lambda_{Na}{/tex} be the corresponding wavelengths of these metals. Then, {tex}\omega_{Cu}=\frac{hc}{\lambda_{Cu}}{/tex} and {tex}\omega_{Na}=\frac{hc}{\lambda_{Na}}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{\omega_{Cu}}{\omega_{Na}}=\frac{hc}{\lambda_{Cu}}\times\frac{\lambda_{Na}}{hc}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{\lambda_{Na}}{\lambda_{Cu}}=\frac{\omega_{Cu}}{\omega_{Na}}=\frac{4\cdot5}{2\cdot3}\approx2{/tex}

questions.question_text: For a given frequency of light and a positive plate potential in the set up below, If the intensity of light is increased then
questions.question_html: For a given frequency of light and a positive plate potential in the set up below, If the intensity of light is increased then
questions.explanation: With increase in intensity, no. of incident photon increases. So more electrons emitted from metal and photo current increases with increase in intensity.

questions.question_text: In various experiments on photo electricity, the stopping potential for a given frequency of the incident radiation is
questions.question_html: In various experiments on photo electricity, the stopping potential for a given frequency of the incident radiation is

options.text: proportional to square of radiation intensity
options.html: proportional to square of radiation intensity

questions.explanation: The stopping potential is defined as the potential necessary to stop any electron even the electron with the most kinetic energy from reaching the other plate. By changing radiation intensity only no. of emitted electron changes, their kinetic energy will not change.

questions.question_text: If the kinetic energy of a free electron doubles, its de-Broglie wavelength changes by the factor
questions.question_html: If the kinetic energy of a free electron doubles, its de-Broglie wavelength changes by the factor
questions.explanation: Let {tex}\lambda{/tex} be de-Broglie wavelength of electron, when its kinetic energy is E. Then, {tex}\lambda=\frac h{\sqrt{2mE}}{/tex} Let {tex}\lambda'{/tex} be de-Broglie wavelength of electron, when its kinetic energy is doubled. Then, {tex}\lambda'=\frac h{\sqrt{2m\times2E}}=\frac1{\sqrt2}\times\frac h{\sqrt{2mE}}=\frac1{\sqrt2}\lambda{/tex}

questions.question_text: According to the Einstein’s model minimum energy needed for the electron to escape from a metal surface having work function {tex}{\phi _0}{/tex} , the electron is emitted with maximum kinetic energy, K max =
questions.question_html: According to the Einstein’s model minimum energy needed for the electron to escape from a metal surface having work function {tex}{\phi _0}{/tex}, the electron is emitted with maximum kinetic energy, Kmax =
questions.explanation: When any photon is incident on a metal surface it gives its total energy to a electron. Some energy(Work Function) is used up in come out from the metal surface and remaining energy is kinetic energy of electron. {tex}E = {\phi _0} + {K_{\max }}{/tex} {tex}{K_{\max }} = E - {\phi _0}{/tex} {tex}{K_{\max }} = h\nu - {\phi _0}{/tex}

questions.question_text: According to the Einstein’s model, the threshold frequency for a metal having work function {tex}{\phi _0}{/tex} is given by
questions.question_html: According to the Einstein’s model, the threshold frequency for a metal having work function {tex}{\phi _0}{/tex} is given by
questions.explanation: Threshold frequency is defined as the minimum frequency of incident light which can cause photo electric emission. Work Function, {tex}{\phi _0} = h{\nu _0}{/tex} Thus, {tex}\nu_0=\frac{\phi_0}{h}{/tex}

questions.question_text: In interaction of radiation with matter, radiation behaves as if it is made up of particles called
questions.question_html: In interaction of radiation with matter, radiation behaves as if it is made up of particles called
questions.explanation: Photon is the small packet of energy. Rest mass of photon is zero but its kinetic mass is not zero. The photon travels with the speed of light.

questions.question_text: Each photon has the same speed but different
questions.question_html: Each photon has the same speed but different
questions.explanation: Each photon travels with the speed of light. Energy of photon is {tex}h\nu {/tex} . {tex}\nu {/tex} is the frequency of photon. Energy depends on frequency.
